title: Timepoint 1 — Post-operative day #1
scenarioBrief: Dorothy Pierce is a 72-year-old woman with poorly-controlled hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and GERD who was admitted on 6/10/2027 with perforated cholecystitis and peritonitis and underwent an urgent open cholecystectomy the same day. It is now post-operative day #1. You are paged by nursing because she was noted to have a brief run of asymptomatic atrial fibrillation on telemetry. From the perspective of her cholecystitis she is clinically improving — afebrile since surgery, tolerating a liquid diet, with normal blood pressure and heart rate and a downtrending leukocytosis. She has no prior echocardiogram and is on no anticoagulation or antiplatelet medication. Review the chart (including the telemetry event documentation and her ECGs) before answering.

chartGate._notes: TP1 visibility: complete chart through POD#1 — historical PCP visits (poorly-controlled HTN, T2DM, A1c 7.6%), the 2026 outpatient cardiology consult (no structural heart disease, no echo performed), admission H&P, CT abd/pelvis (perforated cholecystitis), preop and POD#1 ECGs (both sinus), the operative note, the POD#1 surgery progress note, the POD#1 telemetry event note (the 3 brief AF runs), the POD#1 medicine consult, admission and POD#1 labs (downtrending leukocytosis, normal electrolytes/Mg, no thyroid studies), and the medication list (no anticoagulant/antiplatelet). The POD#2-4 course (including the 2-hour episode) is NOT yet visible.

prompts.question: In addition to continuing telemetry monitoring, would you recommend any additional monitoring, testing, or treatment at this time? If so, what?

essential: Affirms continuing telemetry AND systematically looks for / addresses reversible or secondary triggers of post-operative AF (at least two of: electrolytes especially potassium and magnesium — verify/replete; volume status; pain control; hypoxia; anemia; ongoing inflammation/infection from the recent perforated cholecystitis)
Recommends checking thyroid function (TSH) — note none is on file in the chart
Recommends a transthoracic echocardiogram to evaluate for structural heart disease / LV function / left atrial size — explicitly recognizing she has NO prior echocardiogram
Does NOT start therapeutic anticoagulation at this point for brief (15-20 second), self-terminating, asymptomatic runs on POD#1 (recognizes it is premature / burden is minimal / she is early post-op)
Does NOT start a rate- or rhythm-control drug or attempt cardioversion for asymptomatic, self-terminating, rate-controlled runs
bonus: Quantifies the actual AF burden by reading the telemetry EVENT note (3 runs, each 15-20 seconds, asymptomatic, self-limited) rather than relying on the brief daily progress-note summary

Frames the key uncertainty up front: new AF in the setting of acute illness/recent surgery is often transient/secondary and may not reflect a chronic substrate — this is what ongoing monitoring will clarify
Notes that very brief self-terminating runs generally do not by themselves cross a threshold mandating anticoagulation, but do warrant continued monitoring and risk-factor optimization
Specifically addresses magnesium optimization (even toward high-normal) and treating pain/volume as AF triggers
Mentions reviewing the medication list for proarrhythmic agents and confirming adequate oxygenation
redFlags: Starting therapeutic anticoagulation immediately for 15-20 second self-terminating runs on POD#1
Starting an antiarrhythmic or aggressive rate control, or cardioverting, an asymptomatic self-terminating brief run
Ignoring reversible/secondary causes (no electrolyte or thyroid assessment)
Failing to recognize that no prior echocardiogram exists / not assessing for structural heart disease

scoringRubric.rubricText:
Total 4 points.
- Prophylactic anticoagulation to prevent VTE/DVT (e.g., enoxaparin 40 mg daily): 1 point. Credit for either correct labeling of the intervention OR a correct med + dose combination.
- TSH: 1 point
- Up to 2 of the following (1 point each, max 2):
  - Echocardiogram
  - Electrolyte assessment and/or repletion
  - Evaluation for additional triggers (e.g., pain, constipation)
APPLICABLE MAXIMUM: 4.
